title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs untrusted inputs, integrity, privacy, release, rollback, and defense from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. stimulus.title: Production Defense case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Bookbot team handles case 8716 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Passing the final happy-path demo makes Bookbot production ready.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release. Artifact under review: production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense.

options.text: The Production Defense record says production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Production Defense.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Production Defense for production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense.
The record names the assumption, applies Production Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 1335 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Production Defense through the concrete production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.
